concept.conceptName: Calcium [Moles/volume] corrected for albumin in Serum or Plasma
Calcium [Mass/volume] corrected for albumin in Serum or Plasma
Calcium [Moles/volume] corrected for total protein in Serum or Plasma
Calcium [Mass/volume] corrected for total protein in Serum or Plasma
Calcium [Moles/volume] corrected for total protein in Blood

en.name: Corrected calcium in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Corrected calcium is the total calcium concentration mathematically adjusted to compensate for variations in plasma albumin (or total protein). In hypoalbuminaemia, total calcium falls because the protein-bound fraction decreases, even though the biologically active ionized fraction may be normal — the correction is meant to estimate the calcium concentration the patient would have had with a normal albumin [1, 2]. It is used when ionized calcium is not directly available. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp. Results are reported as a substance concentration in mmol/L (SI unit) or as a mass concentration in mg/dL [3]. **Note: this concept set covers corrected calcium only.** Total calcium (uncorrected) is captured by a separate concept set, "Calcium in blood"; ionized (free) calcium is captured by "Ionized calcium in blood". The three are different measurands and must not be merged. **Note: different correction formulas.** LOINC distinguishes corrections **for albumin** from corrections **for total protein**. The most widely cited albumin-based formula is Payne's (1973) [1]: - US (mg/dL): `Adjusted calcium = calcium − albumin (g/dL) + 4.0` - SI (mmol/L, g/L): `Adjusted calcium = calcium + 0.025 × (40 − albumin g/L)` For correction by total protein, LOINC does not specify a single canonical formula; preserve the source method when known. All forms (albumin-corrected, protein-corrected) are included in this concept set because most clinical sources aggregate them under "corrected calcium", but they are not numerically equivalent. Study designs that explicitly compare formulas should preserve the distinction at the source value level. ## Included Concepts The set lists five LOINC "Lab Test" concepts, grouped here by correction formula. ### Corrected for albumin (Payne's formula) - *3020059 / 29265-6 — Calcium [Moles/volume] corrected for albumin in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: SI form. Most widely used corrected calcium code. - *3047107 / 46099-8 — Calcium [Mass/volume] corrected for albumin in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: same measurand expressed as mass concentration. ### Corrected for total protein - *3004064 / 18281-6 — Calcium [Moles/volume] corrected for total protein in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)* - *3030597 / 34907-6 — Calcium [Mass/volume] corrected for total protein in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: same measurand expressed as mass concentration. - *3049461 / 47597-0 — Calcium [Moles/volume] corrected for total protein in Blood (LOINC)* **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`mmol/L`** (concept 8753 — millimole per litre) for `[Moles/volume]` codes and **`mg/dL`** (concept 8840) for `[Mass/volume]` codes. Conversion: `1 mmol/L ≈ 4.01 mg/dL`. ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded by name. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "corrected calcium", "calcium corrigé", "Ca corrected" with the corrected-for-albumin formula: `3020059 / 29265-6 — Calcium [Moles/volume] corrected for albumin in Serum or Plasma`. - **Source explicitly mentions correction for total protein** (rather than albumin): map to the corresponding `corrected for total protein` LOINC code. Do **not** merge with `corrected for albumin` without checking — the two formulas yield different values. - **Source unit is mg/dL**: convert to mmol/L using `1 mg/dL ≈ 0.2495 mmol/L`. - **Source reports total calcium without correction**: do **not** map. Use "Calcium in blood" instead. - **Source reports ionized calcium**: do **not** map. Use "Ionized calcium in blood" instead. - **Do not confuse with**: total calcium (uncorrected, separate concept set), ionized (free) calcium (separate concept set). ## References [1] Payne RB, Little AJ, Williams RB, Milner JR. Interpretation of serum calcium in patients with abnormal serum proteins. *Br Med J*. 1973;4(5893):643-6. Available: https://doi.org/10.1136/bmj.4.5893.643 [2] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 29265-6 — Calcium [Moles/volume] corrected for albumin in Serum or Plasma. Available: https://loinc.org/29265-6/ [3] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
